blurb: Marriage, Paul says, mirrors a cosmic romance: one Bridegroom, one bride, one unbroken vow. These verses invite every household to become a living parable—two imperfect people learning the rhythm of self-giving that flows from Christ to Church and back again. Let the low notes of forgiveness and the high notes of delight weave a song your neighbors can hear; when love keeps time with the gospel, even the silent rooms sing.

verses.text: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.
Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
